Content data (e.g. browsing logs, email, sms/mms) - Cookies (e.g. Session cookies for internal systems) - Customer care data (internal/external) (e.g. chat logs, email transcripts) - Customer Relationship Management data (e.g. call history, tariff info, service usage data) - Data about the Device and SIM (e.g. sim card number, IMEI, IP address, log data) - Data from IT application/platform (e.g. user activity logs) - Data from the Device (e.g. data collected by apps, user contact lists, logs) - Employment Details (e.g. data available on SF) - Financial data (e.g. account details, credit card details) - Health and Safety Records (e.g. self assessments, work related injury records) - HR Administration and Support (e.g. general correspondence, basic employment information) - Incidents and investigations (e.g. Incident and investigation files) - Law Enforcement Requests (e.g. records of law enforcement requests) - Location data (e.g. Geo location) - Performance and Talent Information (e.g. mid/end year ratings and reviews) - Permissions - Profiling data (e.g. anonymous customer reference, pseudonymous profiles) - Public identification data (e.g. Driving licence, passport) - Recruitment Data (e.g. CV, Application form, reference) - Salary and payment details  - Security data (e.g. event logs) - Special categories of personal data (e.g. health) - Supplier data (e.g. contract, supplier contact data) - Supplier Employee' data (e.g. name, job title) - Traffic Data (e.g. call duration, call logs, IP add) - Visitor Details (e.g. visitor logs - name, time of visit)

Guidance: - Where OFFICIAL information is particularly sensitive and loss or disclosure would have even more damaging consequences, the classification is caveated with “SENSITIVE” i.e. OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE. This information has additional handling requirements to reinforce the principle of ‘need to know’ but is not a classification in its own right. - There is no formal Government definition of OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE information; some customers provide examples, which they, individually, consider to be OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE.  For example - Customer IP addresses, MAC addresses, configuration and design information for boundary and perimeter security including firewall, switch and router configurations - Low level design documentation with unredacted technical infrastructure and network topology diagrams - System fault and change information which includes any information which could assist an attacker, such as security or vulnerability information - Risk registers, risk assessments and remediation plans regarding the security of customer environments or solutions - Contractual negotiation information - There is no Government mandated requirement for data marked as OFFICIAL or data with a handling caveat OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE  to be retained in the UK or managed by people vetted above BPSS. Any such residency or vetting requirement will be a bespoke customer requirement. SOM Governance do not work on any activities which involve assets marked as SECRET or above, these activities are managed within VBSE.
Question: UK SOM Specific: - Does the application contain any official-sensitive information, including: - Customer IP addresses, MAC addresses, configuration and design information for boundary and perimeter security including firewall, switch and router configurations (and does this relation to a standard service or a customer specific service). - Low level design documentation with unredacted technical infrastructure and network topology diagrams (and does this relate to a standard service or a customer specific service) - System fault and change information which includes any information which could assist an attacker, such as security or vulnerability information (and does this relate to a standard service or a customer specific service) - Risk registers, risk assessments and remediation plans regarding the security of customer environments or solutions (and does this relate to a standard service or a customer specific service) - Contract documents
